name: cli-starter
summary: Command-line tool starter: a REAL, running clap-based entrypoint (config loading, standard logging, --version wired to build provenance) plus the release-automation starter pack's working package/release recipes.
post-apply-notes: REAL and RUNNING out of the box (Rust/clap, matching the web/gui/lib/service quick-start family's stack — the only concretely-shipped language across this template set, chosen so `cargo`/`just` recipes are uniform): `cargo build && cargo run -- greet Grimoire` prints a real greeting; `-V`/`--version` prints the crate version plus, once packaged, the grimoire-build-info.json provenance stamp (framework version / build timestamp / source ref, §8) — before a first `just package` it honestly reports a 'dev build, no grimoire-build-info.json found' note rather than fabricating provenance. `src/config.rs` loads a real `config.env` (KEY=VALUE, optional, gitignored) merged under real process env vars. `src/build_info.rs` is the SAME provenance reader shipped in the gui/service quick-start templates (self-contained per-template copy, no shared crate). SEAM: the `greet` subcommand in `src/main.rs` is an illustrative example — replace it with your project's real subcommands; `seed`/`migrate`/`deploy` stay informative placeholders (a one-shot CLI has no data store or live environment by default) — implement them only if your CLI grows a daemon/persistence mode. package/release/smoke are REAL, delegating to the release-automation starter pack's scripts/{package,release}.sh (#431) via scripts/package-manifest.sh — `just package` and `just release` run with ZERO hand-written release code. Pick the language variant matching your project via the `languages` block above (quick-start-template wires it into the CLAUDE.md commands table + the v1.26 code-quality block, not edited inline) — kept for cross-profile consistency even though the shipped payload is concretely Rust. Config defaults are applied via model-effort-profile-switch + workflow-variant-switch.
